file.content:
# From: https://github.com/caarlos0/dotfiles/blob/f9d985c3e8817f8fca133d908a910f173ea933d6/modules/tmux/tmux.conf
# License: MIT

# Color Mode
set -g default-terminal "screen-256color"
setenv -g COLORTERM "truecolor"

set -s escape-time 0
setw -g mouse on

# Fix SSH agent after reconnecting
# https://blog.testdouble.com/posts/2016-11-18-reconciling-tmux-and-ssh-agent-forwarding/
set -g update-environment "DISPLAY SSH_ASKPASS SSH_AGENT_PID SSH_CONNECTION WINDOWID XAUTHORITY WAYLAND_DISPLAY SWAYSOCK"
setenv -g SSH_AUTH_SOCK $HOME/.ssh/ssh_auth_sock

# Ensure window index numbers get reordered on delete.
set-option -g renumber-windows on

# Auto rename tmux window to current cwd
set-option -g status-interval 1
set-option -g automatic-rename on
set-option -g automatic-rename-format '#{b:pane_current_path}'

# Vim-mode copy/paste
set-window-option -g mode-keys vi
bind-key -T copy-mode-vi v send-keys -X begin-selection
bind-key -T copy-mode-vi r send-keys -X rectangle-toggle
bind-key -T copy-mode-vi y send-keys -X copy-pipe-and-cancel 'pbcopy'

# Open splits and windows in the current folder
bind u split-window -l 30% -v -c "#{pane_current_path}"
bind i split-window -l 40% -h -c "#{pane_current_path}"
bind c new-window -c "#{pane_current_path}"

# Repeat time
set-option -g repeat-time 1000

# Clipboard
set -g set-clipboard on

# Status bar
set -g status "on"
set -g status-justify "left"
set -g status-left-length "100"
set -g status-right-length "100"
set -g status-left ""
set -g status-left-style NONE
set -g status-right-style NONE

# Reload tmux config
bind t source-file ~/.config/tmux/tmux.conf

# Default shell
set -gu default-command
set -g default-shell "$SHELL"

# Rename session and window
bind r command-prompt -I "#{window_name}" "rename-window '%%'"
bind R command-prompt -I "#{session_name}" "rename-session '%%'"

# Copy text on select
bind-key -T copy-mode-vi MouseDragEnd1Pane send-keys -X copy-pipe-and-cancel "reattach-to-user-namespace pbcopy"

# Switch window
bind , previous-window
bind . next-window

# Move pane bindings
bind-key h   select-pane -L
bind-key j   select-pane -D
bind-key k   select-pane -U
bind-key l   select-pane -R

# Swap window bindings
bind-key -r "<" swap-window -d -t -1
bind-key -r ">" swap-window -d -t +1

# Resize pane bindings
bind-key -r H resize-pane -L 5
bind-key -r J resize-pane -D 5
bind-key -r K resize-pane -U 5
bind-key -r L resize-pane -R 5

# Split pane bindings
bind-key "|" split-window -h -c "#{pane_current_path}"
bind-key "\\" split-window -fh -c "#{pane_current_path}"
bind-key "-" split-window -v -c "#{pane_current_path}"
bind-key "_" split-window -fv -c "#{pane_current_path}"
bind-key "%" split-window -h -c "#{pane_current_path}"
bind-key '"' split-window -v -c "#{pane_current_path}"

# Yank pane history to clipboard
bind y run-shell 'tmux capture-pane -pS - | pbcopy && tmux display-message "Full pane history copied to clipboard"'
bind v run-shell 'tmux capture-pane -p | pbcopy && tmux display-message "Visible pane copied to clipboard"'

# Pane number indicator
set -g display-panes-colour colour233
set -g display-panes-active-colour colour245

# Clock mode
set -g clock-mode-colour colour39
set -g clock-mode-style 24

# =============================================================================
# Status bar — clean green theme
# =============================================================================
set -g status-style "bg=colour22,fg=colour255"
set -g status-left "#[bg=colour28,fg=colour255,bold] #S #[bg=colour22,fg=colour28] "
set -g status-right "#[fg=colour250]#{?#{pane_current_command},#{pane_current_command},} #[fg=colour28]| #[fg=colour255]%Y/%m/%d %H:%M:%S "
set -g status-left-length 30
set -g status-right-length 60

# Window status
set -g window-status-format " #I:#W "
set -g window-status-current-format "#[bg=colour28,fg=colour255,bold] #I:#W "
set -g window-status-separator ""

# Pane border
set -g pane-border-style "fg=colour238"
set -g pane-active-border-style "fg=colour82,bold"

# Message style
set -g message-style "bg=colour28,fg=colour255"

# =============================================================================
# Plugin configuration
# =============================================================================
# Resurrect + Continuum (session persistence)
set -g @resurrect-capture-pane-contents 'on'
set -g @continuum-restore 'on'
set -g @continuum-save-interval '15'

# Tmux-thumbs (quick text copy)
set -g @thumbs-key Space

# Sessionx (session manager)
set -g @sessionx-bind 'o'

# Tmux sessionizer (fzf session picker)
bind S run-shell "tmux new-window 'fish -c _tss_function'"

# Last session (quick flip)
bind Tab switch-client -l

# Cross-session window picker
bind W run-shell "tmux new-window 'fish -c _tsw_function'"

# Extrakto (text extraction)
set -g @extrakto_key 'tab'

set -g history-limit 0

# Persistent session history logger (starts once per server boot)
run-shell "pgrep -f 'session-logger.sh' > /dev/null 2>&1 || (nohup ~/.config/tmux/session-logger.sh > /dev/null 2>&1 &)"

    39→      rm -f "$old"
    40→    else
    41→      mv "$old" "$ARCHIVE_DIR/${base}--${ts}.txt"
    42→    fi
    43→  done
    44→done

file.content:
#!/usr/bin/env sh
mkdir -p ~/.local/share/tmux/panes
mkdir -p ~/.local/share/tmux/archive
LOG=~/.local/share/tmux/session-history.log
PANE_DIR=~/.local/share/tmux/panes
ARCHIVE_DIR=~/.local/share/tmux/archive

while true; do
  sleep 30

  # Append window metadata
  tmux list-windows -a \
    -F "$(date +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S)  #{session_name}:#{window_index}  #{window_name}  #{pane_current_path}" \
    >>"$LOG" 2>/dev/null

  # Rotate existing live snapshots to .old
  for f in "$PANE_DIR"/*.txt; do
    [ -f "$f" ] && mv "$f" "${f%.txt}.old"
  done

  # Recapture all currently live panes
  tmux list-panes -a -F "#{session_name} #{window_index} #{pane_index} #{pane_id}" \
    2>/dev/null | while IFS= read -r line; do
    sess=$(printf '%s' "$line" | cut -d' ' -f1)
    widx=$(printf '%s' "$line" | cut -d' ' -f2)
    pidx=$(printf '%s' "$line" | cut -d' ' -f3)
    pane_id=$(printf '%s' "$line" | cut -d' ' -f4)
    tmux capture-pane -pt "$pane_id" -S - 2>/dev/null \
      >"$PANE_DIR/$sess--$widx--$pidx.txt"
  done

  # For each .old: if a live .txt exists → pane survived → delete .old
  #                if no live .txt  → pane closed  → archive with timestamp
  ts=$(date +%Y%m%d-%H%M%S)
  for old in "$PANE_DIR"/*.old; do
    [ -f "$old" ] || continue
    base=$(basename "${old%.old}")
    if [ -f "$PANE_DIR/$base.txt" ]; then
      rm -f "$old"
    else
      mv "$old" "$ARCHIVE_DIR/${base}--${ts}.txt"
    fi
  done
done

content.text:
Yes, everything is running correctly. Here's the status:

**Process:** `session-logger.sh` is alive (PID 22055), launched via `run-shell` in `tmux.conf` on server boot.

**Directories:**
- `~/.local/share/tmux/panes/` — 4 live pane captures (refreshed every 30s)
- `~/.local/share/tmux/archive/` — 0 archived panes (no closed panes yet)
- `~/.local/share/tmux/session-history.log` — window metadata being appended every 30s

**What's being captured:**
- `session-history.log` — timestamped list of all windows with session name, index, window name, and cwd
- `panes/<session>--<window>--<pane>.txt` — full scrollback capture of each live pane (`-S -` = entire history)
- `archive/` — when a pane closes, its last snapshot is moved there with a timestamp suffix

Everything is working as designed from the `#926` commit.

content.thinking:
The user is asking whether the current tmux session logging setup scales well. Let me think about potential scaling issues:

1. **`history-limit 0`** — This is set in tmux.conf (`set -g history-limit 0`), which means unlimited scrollback history. Combined with `tmux capture-pane -S -` (which captures the entire history), this could produce extremely large pane capture files over time.

2. **Every 30 seconds**, every pane's full history is written to disk. If you have many panes open for a long time with lots of output, these files grow unboundedly.

3. **The `.old` rotation** — every cycle, live files get renamed to `.old`, then recaptured fresh. 

The pane files themselves don't accumulate since they're overwritten each cycle, but the real issue is that every 30-second interval captures the entire scrollback history. The archive directory grows unboundedly as panes close, and session-history.log keeps appending indefinitely without any rotation mechanism. With multiple tmux sessions, windows, and panes, the I/O overhead and file count scale linearly, which becomes problematic at scale.

The core scaling problems boil down to a few things: setting `history-limit 0` means pane captures can grow to gigabytes if you have long-running processes like build logs, the session history log has no rotation so it balloons over time, the archive lacks any cleanup or TTL, and the constant full-scrollback reads every 30 seconds across many panes creates significant disk I/O. Even the shell glob pattern for iterating through thousands of files could become a bottleneck.

content.text:
A few concrete scaling problems with the current setup:

**1. `history-limit 0` + `capture-pane -S -` is the biggest issue**

`tmux.conf:151` sets unlimited scrollback. `session-logger.sh:28` captures the *entire* history every 30 seconds per pane. A long-running pane (build logs, `claude`, `tail -f`) accumulates indefinitely — and gets fully re-read + re-written to disk every 30s. One busy pane could be GBs after weeks.

**2. `session-history.log` grows forever**

`session-logger.sh:12-14` appends every 30 seconds with no rotation or size cap. After a few months of continuous use this becomes a large file with mostly redundant data.

**3. Archive directory has no TTL or cleanup**

`session-logger.sh:41` moves closed pane captures to `archive/` but nothing ever deletes them. Every closed pane adds a file permanently.

**4. I/O cost scales with pane count × history size**

Every 30s cycle: N file renames + N full-history reads + N full writes. With 20 panes that have large histories, you're doing substantial disk I/O on a tight loop.

**5. Shell glob on `$PANE_DIR/*.txt` / `*.old`**

`session-logger.sh:17,35` — fine for a handful of files, but with hundreds of archived/live panes the shell glob expansion becomes slow.

---

**Quick fixes that would help most:**

- Cap `history-limit` (e.g. `50000` lines) — limits per-pane capture size
- Add `logrotate` or a size check on `session-history.log`
- Add an archive TTL (e.g. delete anything older than 7 days)
- Capture only visible pane (`-p` without `-S -`) if full history isn't needed

Want me to implement any of these?

content.thinking:
The user wants me to create a PR with fixes for the scaling issues identified. Let me implement the fixes first, then create a PR.

The fixes I'll make:
1. Cap `history-limit` to something reasonable (e.g., 50000) in tmux.conf
2. Update `session-logger.sh` to:
   - Add size-based log rotation for `session-history.log`
   - Add TTL cleanup for archive (e.g., 7 days)
   - Use visible-only capture or add a reasonable cap

Let me think about what changes to make:

1. **`tmux.conf`**: Change `set -g history-limit 0` to `set -g history-limit 50000`


2. **`session-logger.sh`**: I'll add log rotation logic that checks if the session history log exceeds 10MB and rotates it, plus a cleanup routine to remove archived logs older than 7 days. The history limit cap will naturally bound the size of what gets captured.

Let me make these changes now.

For `tmux.conf`, I need to update the history limit from 0 to 50000.

toolUseResult.originalFile:
# From: https://github.com/caarlos0/dotfiles/blob/f9d985c3e8817f8fca133d908a910f173ea933d6/modules/tmux/tmux.conf
# License: MIT

# Color Mode
set -g default-terminal "screen-256color"
setenv -g COLORTERM "truecolor"

set -s escape-time 0
setw -g mouse on

# Fix SSH agent after reconnecting
# https://blog.testdouble.com/posts/2016-11-18-reconciling-tmux-and-ssh-agent-forwarding/
set -g update-environment "DISPLAY SSH_ASKPASS SSH_AGENT_PID SSH_CONNECTION WINDOWID XAUTHORITY WAYLAND_DISPLAY SWAYSOCK"
setenv -g SSH_AUTH_SOCK $HOME/.ssh/ssh_auth_sock

# Ensure window index numbers get reordered on delete.
set-option -g renumber-windows on

# Auto rename tmux window to current cwd
set-option -g status-interval 1
set-option -g automatic-rename on
set-option -g automatic-rename-format '#{b:pane_current_path}'

# Vim-mode copy/paste
set-window-option -g mode-keys vi
bind-key -T copy-mode-vi v send-keys -X begin-selection
bind-key -T copy-mode-vi r send-keys -X rectangle-toggle
bind-key -T copy-mode-vi y send-keys -X copy-pipe-and-cancel 'pbcopy'

# Open splits and windows in the current folder
bind u split-window -l 30% -v -c "#{pane_current_path}"
bind i split-window -l 40% -h -c "#{pane_current_path}"
bind c new-window -c "#{pane_current_path}"

# Repeat time
set-option -g repeat-time 1000

# Clipboard
set -g set-clipboard on

# Status bar
set -g status "on"
set -g status-justify "left"
set -g status-left-length "100"
set -g status-right-length "100"
set -g status-left ""
set -g status-left-style NONE
set -g status-right-style NONE

# Reload tmux config
bind t source-file ~/.config/tmux/tmux.conf

# Default shell
set -gu default-command
set -g default-shell "$SHELL"

# Rename session and window
bind r command-prompt -I "#{window_name}" "rename-window '%%'"
bind R command-prompt -I "#{session_name}" "rename-session '%%'"

# Copy text on select
bind-key -T copy-mode-vi MouseDragEnd1Pane send-keys -X copy-pipe-and-cancel "reattach-to-user-namespace pbcopy"

# Switch window
bind , previous-window
bind . next-window

# Move pane bindings
bind-key h   select-pane -L
bind-key j   select-pane -D
bind-key k   select-pane -U
bind-key l   select-pane -R

# Swap window bindings
bind-key -r "<" swap-window -d -t -1
bind-key -r ">" swap-window -d -t +1

# Resize pane bindings
bind-key -r H resize-pane -L 5
bind-key -r J resize-pane -D 5
bind-key -r K resize-pane -U 5
bind-key -r L resize-pane -R 5

# Split pane bindings
bind-key "|" split-window -h -c "#{pane_current_path}"
bind-key "\\" split-window -fh -c "#{pane_current_path}"
bind-key "-" split-window -v -c "#{pane_current_path}"
bind-key "_" split-window -fv -c "#{pane_current_path}"
bind-key "%" split-window -h -c "#{pane_current_path}"
bind-key '"' split-window -v -c "#{pane_current_path}"

# Yank pane history to clipboard
bind y run-shell 'tmux capture-pane -pS - | pbcopy && tmux display-message "Full pane history copied to clipboard"'
bind v run-shell 'tmux capture-pane -p | pbcopy && tmux display-message "Visible pane copied to clipboard"'

# Pane number indicator
set -g display-panes-colour colour233
set -g display-panes-active-colour colour245

# Clock mode
set -g clock-mode-colour colour39
set -g clock-mode-style 24

# =============================================================================
# Status bar — clean green theme
# =============================================================================
set -g status-style "bg=colour22,fg=colour255"
set -g status-left "#[bg=colour28,fg=colour255,bold] #S #[bg=colour22,fg=colour28] "
set -g status-right "#[fg=colour250]#{?#{pane_current_command},#{pane_current_command},} #[fg=colour28]| #[fg=colour255]%Y/%m/%d %H:%M:%S "
set -g status-left-length 30
set -g status-right-length 60

# Window status
set -g window-status-format " #I:#W "
set -g window-status-current-format "#[bg=colour28,fg=colour255,bold] #I:#W "
set -g window-status-separator ""

# Pane border
set -g pane-border-style "fg=colour238"
set -g pane-active-border-style "fg=colour82,bold"

# Message style
set -g message-style "bg=colour28,fg=colour255"

# =============================================================================
# Plugin configuration
# =============================================================================
# Resurrect + Continuum (session persistence)
set -g @resurrect-capture-pane-contents 'on'
set -g @continuum-restore 'on'
set -g @continuum-save-interval '15'

# Tmux-thumbs (quick text copy)
set -g @thumbs-key Space

# Sessionx (session manager)
set -g @sessionx-bind 'o'

# Tmux sessionizer (fzf session picker)
bind S run-shell "tmux new-window 'fish -c _tss_function'"

# Last session (quick flip)
bind Tab switch-client -l

# Cross-session window picker
bind W run-shell "tmux new-window 'fish -c _tsw_function'"

# Extrakto (text extraction)
set -g @extrakto_key 'tab'

set -g history-limit 0

# Persistent session history logger (starts once per server boot)
run-shell "pgrep -f 'session-logger.sh' > /dev/null 2>&1 || (nohup ~/.config/tmux/session-logger.sh > /dev/null 2>&1 &)"

lines: # Persistent session history logger (starts once per server boot)

content.text: Now update `session-logger.sh` to add log rotation and archive TTL cleanup:

input.content:
#!/usr/bin/env sh
mkdir -p ~/.local/share/tmux/panes
mkdir -p ~/.local/share/tmux/archive
LOG=~/.local/share/tmux/session-history.log
PANE_DIR=~/.local/share/tmux/panes
ARCHIVE_DIR=~/.local/share/tmux/archive
LOG_MAX_BYTES=10485760  # 10 MB
ARCHIVE_TTL_DAYS=7

while true; do
  sleep 30

  # Rotate session log if it exceeds LOG_MAX_BYTES
  if [ -f "$LOG" ] && [ "$(wc -c < "$LOG")" -gt "$LOG_MAX_BYTES" ]; then
    mv "$LOG" "${LOG}.old"
  fi

  # Append window metadata
  tmux list-windows -a \
    -F "$(date +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S)  #{session_name}:#{window_index}  #{window_name}  #{pane_current_path}" \
    >>"$LOG" 2>/dev/null

  # Rotate existing live snapshots to .old
  for f in "$PANE_DIR"/*.txt; do
    [ -f "$f" ] && mv "$f" "${f%.txt}.old"
  done

  # Recapture all currently live panes
  tmux list-panes -a -F "#{session_name} #{window_index} #{pane_index} #{pane_id}" \
    2>/dev/null | while IFS= read -r line; do
    sess=$(printf '%s' "$line" | cut -d' ' -f1)
    widx=$(printf '%s' "$line" | cut -d' ' -f2)
    pidx=$(printf '%s' "$line" | cut -d' ' -f3)
    pane_id=$(printf '%s' "$line" | cut -d' ' -f4)
    tmux capture-pane -pt "$pane_id" -S - 2>/dev/null \
      >"$PANE_DIR/$sess--$widx--$pidx.txt"
  done

  # For each .old: if a live .txt exists → pane survived → delete .old
  #                if no live .txt  → pane closed  → archive with timestamp
  ts=$(date +%Y%m%d-%H%M%S)
  for old in "$PANE_DIR"/*.old; do
    [ -f "$old" ] || continue
    base=$(basename "${old%.old}")
    if [ -f "$PANE_DIR/$base.txt" ]; then
      rm -f "$old"
    else
      mv "$old" "$ARCHIVE_DIR/${base}--${ts}.txt"
    fi
  done

  # Purge archive files older than ARCHIVE_TTL_DAYS
  find "$ARCHIVE_DIR" -name '*.txt' -mtime +"$ARCHIVE_TTL_DAYS" -delete 2>/dev/null
done

content.content: The file /Users/shunkakinoki/dotfiles/home-manager/programs/tmux/session-logger.sh has been updated successfully.

  done

  # For each .old: if a live .txt exists → pane survived → delete .old
  #                if no live .txt  → pane closed  → archive with timestamp
  ts=$(date +%Y%m%d-%H%M%S)
  for old in "$PANE_DIR"/*.old; do
    [ -f "$old" ] || continue
    base=$(basename "${old%.old}")
    if [ -f "$PANE_DIR/$base.txt" ]; then
      rm -f "$old"
    else
      mv "$old" "$ARCHIVE_DIR/${base}--${ts}.txt"
    fi
  done

  # Purge archive files older than ARCHIVE_TTL_DAYS
  find "$ARCHIVE_DIR" -name '*.txt' -mtime +"$ARCHIVE_TTL_DAYS" -delete 2>/dev/null
done

content.text: Both files are back to their original state. So what do you actually want in the PR — just confirming the setup is correct as-is, or is there something else you wanted to improve?
